_meta.description: D6 fixtures for hermes / headless-complete (probe featureType gen-ui-headless-complete, route /demos/headless-complete). Full hand-rolled headless surface. Three client-executed frontend tools via useFrontendTool with deterministic fake-data handlers that mirror the langgraph-python headless_complete backend return shapes 1:1 (get_weather → WeatherCard, get_stock_price → StockCard, get_revenue_chart → ChartCard) + a useComponent frontend tool (highlight_note → HighlightNote). The fixture makes the agent EMIT each tool call; the client handler produces the result; the per-tool renderer paints the card. Two-leg pattern per tool: first leg gated on toolName emits the tool call (fires whenever the tool is registered, independent of prior thread history); second leg gated on toolCallId narrates AFTER the tool result lands. Second (narration) leg placed BEFORE the first (emit) leg in the array — aimock is first-match-wins, and the toolCallId key is uniquely satisfied only once the tool has run. highlight_note is a useComponent (UI-only) tool so its render IS the result; it still uses the same two-leg pattern. D5 probe prompts (verbatim SuggestionBar `.message` from use-headless-suggestions.ts): "What's the weather in Tokyo?", "What's the price of AAPL right now?", "Highlight this note for me: 'ship the demo on Friday'.", "Show me a chart of revenue over the last six months." Narration strings kept 1:1 with langgraph-python's headless-complete e2e assertions where applicable.
_meta.sourceFile: langgraph-python headless-complete backend tools + d5-gen-ui-headless-complete probe

fixtures._comment: Weather in Tokyo — narration leg. Fires after get_weather ran (keyed on the emitted toolCallId). MUST precede the emit leg below (first-match-wins).

response.content: Tokyo is 22°C and partly cloudy. The headless WeatherCard above shows the snapshot from the tool.

fixtures._comment: Weather in Tokyo — emit leg: emit get_weather(Tokyo). Gated on toolName so it fires whenever the tool is registered, regardless of prior tool results in a multi-pill thread.
AAPL price — narration leg. Fires after get_stock_price ran.

response.content: AAPL is trading at $189.42, up 1.27% on the day. The card above shows the live ticker and the percentage change.

fixtures._comment: AAPL price — emit leg: emit get_stock_price(AAPL). Gated on toolName.
Highlight note — narration leg. Fires after the highlight_note useComponent tool call landed.

response.content: Highlighted 'ship the demo on Friday' in yellow above. Let me know if you want a different color or a longer note.

fixtures._comment: Highlight note — emit leg: emit highlight_note (useComponent frontend tool; its render IS the result). Gated on toolName.
Revenue chart — narration leg. Fires after get_revenue_chart ran.

match.userMessage: revenue over the last six months

response.content: Here is the chart of revenue over the last six months — quarterly revenue grew from $38K in January to $71K in June, with one dip in April.

fixtures._comment: Revenue chart — emit leg: emit get_revenue_chart (no args). Gated on toolName. Includes a short leading `content` snippet so the word 'revenue' lands in the assistant bubble synchronously with the tool-call emission — without it the chart card title falls back to 'Chart' during the `executing` render phase (result still undefined) and the D5 probe's 'revenue' token can miss on the first settle window before the narration follow-up arrives (mirrors langgraph-python's fixture).

match.userMessage: revenue over the last six months

response.content: Pulling the revenue chart for the last six months...
